steps.title: Connect bones to climbing
steps.content: The scientists infer that Little Foot adapted to life in trees by comparing its fossilized clavicle and shoulder bones with those of modern climbing apes. The blank must express a similarity that supports that inference.

steps.title: Reject illogical relationships
steps.content: Choice A suggests Little Foot's bones were inferior, which would not support climbing adaptation. Choice C says the bones were unrelated to the ape bones. Choice D suggests the fossils came from modern apes, which is impossible for 3.6-million-year-old remains. The needed phrase means similar to. The answer is B.
